PART 4: ACKNOWLEDGEMENT AND AGREEMENT
第四部份：知悉及同意
By signing below, I, the Policyowner, fully understand and agree with the following:
1. ‘Policyowner’ and ‘Designated Person’ refers to the individuals (i.e. a natural person) named in this Form. For avoidance of doubt,
the term ‘Policyowner’ shall carry the same meaning as ‘Policy Holder’ per relevant Endorsement.
2. Insurance Advisor cannot be the Designated Person, unless he/she is my immediate family member.
3. If a Successive Policyowner has been nominated for the Policy and Option 1 under Incapacity Care Service has been chosen, the
Designated Person must be the same person as the Successive Policyowner.
4. The Designated Person must have attained the age of 18 at the time he/she is appointed as a Designated Person.
5. The Incapacity Care Service arrangement (“Incapacity Care Service Arrangement”) shall include, but not be limited to, (1) the appointment or
change of the Designated Person, and (2) the policy management instruction (i.e. Option 1 or Option 2) (‘Policy Management Instruction’).
6. The Incapacity Care Service does not constitute an enduring power of attorney (‘EPOA’) (applicable to Hong Kong) or a Power of
Attorney (applicable to Macao), a guardianship order, or a committee order, and does not appoint the Designated Person as the
Policyowner’s attorney, guardian, or committee. In the event of a dispute between the Designated Person and any other person,
including but not limited to the Policyowner's guardian or committee, attorney (including an attorney appointed under a
pre-existing EPOA) or beneficiary(ies), or there are inconsistencies or conflicts between the instructions given by the Designated
Person or any other person, including but not limited to the Policyowner's guardian or committee, attorney (including an attorney
appointed under a pre-existing EPOA) or beneficiary(ies), the Company shall treat this as a dispute and the Company reserves the
right to suspend the execution of Policy Management Instruction until such dispute is legally resolved. The Company shall not be
liable for any loss, damages and/or costs incurred arising out of such dispute, the suspension of the execution, and/or the
execution of the Incapacity Care Service Arrangement or any Policy Management Instructions.
7. The transfer of Policy ownership to the Designated Person under Option 1 pursuant to term 8 below, will only become effective
upon the completion of customer due diligence to the satisfaction of the Company in accordance with the requirements of legal
and/or regulatory bodies, including but not limited to the Insurance Authority (applicable to Hong Kong) and Monetary Authority
of Macao (applicable to Macao), with respect to the prevention of money laundering and terrorist financing and other applicable
guidelines. The transfer will also be subject to the Company’s receipt of any other documents as requested by the Company and
the prevailing administrative rules of the Company.
8. Subject to terms 7, 9, 10 and 11 hereof and satisfactory evidence of the Policyowner’s diagnosis of the Designated Illness (as defined below)
together with any other necessary documents as prescribed by the Company from time to time, provided to the Company's satisfaction:
8.1 If the pre-selected Policy Management Instruction is Option 1, the Designated Person will become the new Policyowner of the
Policy upon the diagnosis of the Policyowner with a Designated Illness and shall assume all obligations (including but not
limited to satisfying any premium payment obligations) and be bound by and subject to the terms and conditions of the Policy.
8.2 If the pre-selected Policy Management Instruction is Option 2, the withdrawal amount that will be paid to the Designated Person
equals to (1) surrender value multiplied by the percentage designated by the Policyowner (‘Designated Amount’); and (2) less any
Indebtedness. The Designated Amount will be withdrawn first from (1) balance of accumulated guaranteed benefits (if any), including
but not limited to any guaranteed cash payments, and subsequently be withdrawn from (2) balance of accumulated non-guaranteed
benefits (if any), including but not limited to any annual dividends, non-guaranteed income and realized terminal bonus or realized
special bonus (if any). If the sum of the balance of accumulated guaranteed benefits (if any) and the balance of accumulated
non-guaranteed benefits (if any) is less than the Designated Amount, the remaining portion of Designated Amount will be withdrawn
from (3) guaranteed cash value and non-guaranteed terminal bonus or non-guaranteed cash value of special bonus (if any) in
proportion through reduction of notional amount. A reduction of notional amount is subject to applicable minimum notional amount
and/or premium requirements. Kindly note that the aforementioned components for calculating the total withdrawal amount may
not be applicable to your Policy. When the withdrawal results in reaching of the minimum notional amount and/or premium, the
actual withdrawal amount may be less than the amount calculated based on the percentage of surrender value as designated by the
Policyowner. Policy surrender will be triggered automatically if the percentage designated by the Policyowner is 100% and the
withdrawal amount will be paid in the amount of surrender value after deducting any Indebtedness. The Policy will then be
terminated automatically on the approval date of such withdrawal request. If a committee or guardian is appointed under the Mental
Health Ordinance (Cap. 136, Laws of Hong Kong SAR) or under the Macau Civil Code (or if there is a valid committee or guardian
appointed under similar laws in another jurisdiction), payment will only be made to the Designated Person with the consent of the
committee or guardian, as the case may be.
Once the policy ownership transfer to the Designated Person or the one-off withdrawal is approved by the Company, it cannot be
changed or revoked.
The Incapacity Care Service Arrangement will be terminated when the Designated Person becomes the new Policyowner of the
Policy or the withdrawal amount is paid to the Designated Person (as the case may be).
The Policyowner acknowledges and confirms that the actions of the Designated Person carried out under the Policy Management
Instruction shall be binding on the Policyowner in the event that the Policyowner should subsequently recover from the
Designated Illness (defined below), and that such binding nature of the Designated Person’s actions shall survive the termination
of the Incapacity Care Service Arrangement.
9. Any appointment of a Designated Person under the Policy will be revoked automatically upon (1) the Company’s acceptance of the
change of policy ownership, (2) the Company is being notified that the Policyowner has created an EPOA (applicable to Hong
Kong) or has executed a Power of Attorney (applicable to Macao) covering the Policy, or (3) the Company is being notified of a
committee or guardianship order in respect of the Policyowner covering the Policy taking effect, or (4) the Company’s acceptance
of a subsequent arrangement of policy assignment, or (5) the Company’s acceptance of the application of premium holiday.
10. While the Policyowner is mentally sane, he/she shall be responsible to promptly inform the Company if,
(a) Designated Person predeceases the Policyowner, or
(b) Designated Person provides written confirmation to the Policyowner that he/she is unwilling to accept the appointment, or
(c) Designated Person is incapable of accepting the appointment.
Upon the occurrence of any of the above, the appointment of a Designated Person will be invalidated with immediate effect as if
such appointment has never been made and the Policyowner shall retain the ownership of the Policy accordingly.

11. At any time after the Policyowner’s diagnosis of the Designated Illness and before the Designated Person executes the Policy
Management Instruction, the appointment of the Designated Person shall be invalidated with immediate effect as if such
appointment has never been made if (a) the Designated Person predeceases the Policyowner, or (b) the Policyowner predeceases
the submission of the application for execution of the Policy Management Instruction by the Designated Person and the same
being received by the Company, or (c) if the Designated Person provides written confirmation to the Company that he/she is
unwilling to accept the appointment, or (d) the Designated Person is incapable of accepting the appointment, or (e) application
for execution of the Policy Management Instruction has not been submitted by Designated Person and received by the Company
within 365 calendar days from the date Designated Illness was first diagnosed.
12. In the event the appointment of the Designated Person is invalidated under term 11(b) hereof, the Policy shall become part of the
estate of the deceased Policyowner. The executor or administrator of the estate shall manage the Policy in accordance with the
terms of the deceased Policyowner's will, the applicable intestacy laws, and the Company's administrative rules. The estate shall
be responsible for paying the premiums and fulfilling other obligations under the Policy, and any benefits or proceeds from the
Policy shall be distributed as part of the estate. In such cases, the new policyowner shall be determined through the relevant
probate process. In the event the appointment of the Designated Person is invalidated under terms 11(a), (c)-(e), the Policyowner
shall in such an event be entitled to retain the ownership of the Policy upon providing proof to the satisfaction of the Company.
13. The “Designated Illness” stated in this Form means each of which defined below, provided that the diagnosis and severity of all
medical conditions as referred to below must be supported by appropriate test results and investigations, including but not limited to,
clinical, radiological, histological, and laboratory evidence, to the satisfaction of the Company, by a Specialist Medical Practitioner:
13.1 Apallic syndrome – universal necrosis of the brain cortex, with the brainstem remaining intact. Diagnosis must be confirmed
by a Specialist Medical Practitioner who is a neurologist and condition must be documented for at least one (1) month.
13.2 Coma – A state of unconsciousness with no reaction to external stimuli or internal needs, persisting continuously with the
use of mechanical life support systems for a period of at least 96 hours and resulting in a permanent neurological deficit
confirmed by a Specialist Medical Practitioner who is a neurologist. Coma caused by alcohol or drug use is excluded.
13.3 Loss of independent existence – A state of inability for the Policyowner to perform any 3 of the 6 Activities of Daily Living
and requiring the physical assistance of another person throughout the entire activity. Such condition should be beyond the
hope of recovery with current medical knowledge and technology. All psychiatric related causes are excluded from this
definition of loss of independent existence.
13.4 Major head trauma – Accidental head injury causing significant and permanent functional impairment which has lasted for a
minimum period of three (3) months from the date of the trauma or injury. The resultant significant permanent functional
impairment must be confirmed by a Specialist Medical Practitioner who is a neurologist. For purpose of the definition of
“Major head trauma”, the word “Accidental” shall mean an unforeseen and unexpected event.
13.5 Mental incapacity – The Policyowner is incapable, by reason of mental incapacity, of managing and administering his or her
property and affairs. This condition must be confirmed by two Doctors (one is required to be a registered psychiatrist or
neurologist and one is the Policyowner’s attending Doctor) by issuing medical reports.
13.6 Paralysis – The total and irreversible loss of use of two or more limbs through paralysis.
13.7 Terminal illness – The suffering by the Policyowner from a disease which, in the opinion of a Doctor and agreed to by the
Company’s Doctor, the advent of death is highly likely within twelve (12) months.
“Activities of Daily Living” as stated in this Form shall mean:
i) Washing - the ability to wash in the bath or shower (including getting into and out of the bath or shower) or wash
satisfactorily by other means.
ii) Dressing - the ability to put on, take off, secure and unfasten all garments and, as appropriate, any braces, artificial limbs or
other surgical appliances.
iii) Transferring - the ability to move from a bed to an upright chair or wheelchair and vice versa.
iv) Mobility - the ability to move indoors from room to room on level surfaces.
v) Toileting - the ability to use the lavatory or otherwise manage bowel and bladder functions so as to maintain a satisfactory
level of personal hygiene.
vi) Feeding - the ability to feed oneself once food has been prepared and made available.
If the activity can be performed by using special equipment, then the Policyowner will be considered able to perform that activity.
“Doctor” as stated in this Form shall mean a registered medical practitioner qualified by degree in western medicine who is legally
authorized by the government of the geographical area of his or her practice to render medical and surgical services other than dental
related services, but exclude any such person who is the Designated Person, Life Insured or Policyowner of this Policy, or the business
partner(s), insurance agent or employer/employee of the Designated Person, Policyowner or the Life Insured, or any of the immediate family
member of either the Designated Person, Life Insured or Policyowner unless such person is first approved by the Company in writing.
“Specialist Medical Practitioner” as stated in this Form shall mean a Doctor who is registered with or recognized by the relevant authority in
his or her geographical area of practice as specialized in the area of medicine under which the Designated Illness claimed is managed. The
Specialist Medical Practitioner must be o

Remarks on Part B
第二部份註釋
Please consider the followings when applying for the change of autopay arrangement:
申請更改自動轉帳安排時，請考慮以下事項：
(1) The policy will be terminated in the event of failure to pay outstanding regular premium due during mandatory contribution period in full upon expiration of the grace
period, in which the policy will be subject to surrender charge.
若未能於寬限期屆滿時全數繳付於必要供款期內到期未付的定期保費，此保單將會終止，而在此情況下此保單須支付退保費用。
(2) After the mandatory contribution period and during the premium payment period, the policy will automatically enter a premium suspension when the Company does not
receive regular premium in full by the respective premium due date as long as the account value is sufficient to cover the monthly deduction due. If the account value is
insufficient to cover the outstanding monthly deduction due during premium suspension, grace period is not available, and the policy will immediately be terminated.
於必要供款期後及保費繳付期內，當本公司未能於相應保費到期日或之前收到全數的定期保費，只要戶口價值足夠支付到期每月費用，保單會自動行使暫停繳付
保費。於暫停繳付保費期間，若戶口價值不足夠支付到期未付的每月費用，將不會設有寬限期，以及保單將立即終止。

Remarks on Part C
第三部份註釋
(1) Please note that no more than one switch instruction (regardless of whether it is given by whatever means) should be given to us with respect to the SAME
switch option for the SAME POLICY on the SAME DAY (cut-off time is 3:00 p.m. Hong Kong time). If more than one SAME switching instruction with respect to
the same policy is received by us on the same day, we may, but are not obliged to, contact you to clarify your instruction. This may lead to delay in our
processing of your instruction. For the avoidance of doubt, we shall have the sole right and discretion to accept or reject your subsequent instructions if you give
SAME switching instructions with respect to the same policy more than once to us on the same day.
請勿於同一日內（截至香港時間下午三時止）就同一份保單提交相同的轉換指示多於一次（無論透過任何方式）。如公司於同一日內超過一次收到有關同一份保單
的相同轉換指示，我們可能（但並非必須）聯絡閣下以作確認，因此閣下的指示或會延遲處理。為清晰起見，如公司在同一日內接到閣下就同一份保單提交相同轉
換指示多於一次，我們有權接受或拒絕受理其後的相同轉換指示。
(2) The minimum switching amount is USD250 for each switching from one investment choice to another, unless all units under the investment choice is being switched out.
每次從一項投資選項轉換至另一項投資選項的最低轉換金額為250美元，除非該投資選項的所有單位會被轉出。
(3) The actual number of units switched out may be less than the number of units you indicated due to insufficient units after fees and charges deduction prior to switching out.
實際轉出的單位數目或會因於轉出前扣除費用及收費後所引致的單位不足而少於閣下指示的單位數目。
